metadata.purpose: Source coverage plan for closing pre-match prediction data gaps.

dimensions.refresh_cadence: Daily, plus immediately after final whistle for tracked matches.
dimensions.prediction_use: Match context, lifecycle status, result records, and review triggers through reviewed Match 073 and predicted Match 076.

dimensions.refresh_cadence: At T-72h, T-24h, and when official squad or lineup updates appear.
dimensions.prediction_use: Squad quality, depth, key-player availability, likely lineup quality.

fallback_sources: beat reporters with direct federation access
dimensions.refresh_cadence: T-24h team-news pass, T-6h availability pass, and T-1h lineup pass.
dimensions.prediction_use: Confidence gate, late risk factors, scoreline-scenario adjustment.

primary_sources: National Weather Service for United States venues
Environment and Climate Change Canada for Canada venues
Mexico national meteorological service for Mexico venues
dimensions.refresh_cadence: T-72h baseline, T-24h forecast refresh, T-6h match-condition refresh.
dimensions.prediction_use: Tempo, fatigue, set-piece variance, finishing quality, draw risk, cooling-break effects, and late-stadium heat/shade effects.

primary_sources: bookmaker consensus snapshots where legally accessible
fallback_sources: reputable previews that cite current market prices
The Odds API or equivalent licensed feed if configured
dimensions.refresh_cadence: T-72h opening snapshot, T-24h movement snapshot, T-6h final movement snapshot.
dimensions.prediction_use: Calibration signal only; never betting advice or proof of outcome.

dimensions.refresh_cadence: T-72h baseline and T-24h disagreement pass.
dimensions.prediction_use: Dissenting case, tactical matchup, public narrative, psychology, late formation surprises, and substitute-impact scenarios.

primary_sources: FIFA match centre group tables and bracket pages
dimensions.refresh_cadence: Every prediction run, plus immediately after any same-group or same-bracket result changes the path.
dimensions.prediction_use: Compare win/draw/loss paths for qualification, group rank, likely next-round opponent strength, travel/rest burden, draw quadrant, rotation risk, and strategic path-selection hypotheses. For current Round of 32 predictions, losing has no next-round benefit, so the analysis focuses on win-path management.

fallback_sources: manual review tags from daily automation
dimensions.prediction_use: Calibration feedback through Match 073 for draw floor, favorite margin, clean-sheet, underdog resilience, transition routes, set-piece risk, host pressure, knockout extra-time risk, and bracket-path management.
